description: Leaving non-deterministic sources (unseeded RNG, hash iteration order, wall-clock, variable dt) in the simulation, causing replay and lockstep desyncs.
body: Failure mode: the simulation uses unseeded randomness, hash-map iteration order, wall-clock timing, or variable timestep, so the same inputs produce different states on different runs or machines. Detection signals: replays diverge from the original; lockstep multiplayer desyncs after a while, often only on certain hardware; per-tick state checksums differ. Do not rely on an accidentally-deterministic sim. Safer fallback: fix the timestep, seed and advance RNG deterministically, use stable iteration order, avoid wall-clock and platform-divergent float math, and validate with per-tick checksums across machines.
